refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for each phase of the load→travel→dump cycle to meet the customer-defined target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-FRs for each phase allows targeted design and verification of each step, improving clarity and traceability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for load, travel, and dump phases separately under representative duty cycles and site conditions.
sub_frs: Minimize load phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize travel phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize dump phase cycle time to meet target

candidate_dps: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for each phase
Implement adaptive teleoperation control to reduce delays
Integrate safety-aware cycle time optimization algorithms
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for each phase

potential_sources: Interaction between load and travel phase controls
Safety constraints impacting cycle time optimization
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically decoupled by phase with safety constraints handled separately, minimizing cross-phase coupling.

assumptions_risks: Vehicle control systems can be optimized independently per phase
Safety constraints will not overly restrict cycle time improvements
Teleoperation latency is minimal or handled separately
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating teleoperation and safety optimization in subsequent decomposition levels.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for executing load, travel, and dump phases efficiently.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends cycle time and status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands from NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle module fully contains mechanical and control systems for cycle time optimization.
NGHE Solution oversees overall coordination but does not directly implement cycle time controls.
Teleoperation impacts cycle time indirectly via control commands but is not primary implementer.
Safety Services influence cycle time through constraints but do not implement cycle time logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the primary owner of cycle time optimization DPs, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation commands causing diffusion of control logic
Safety constraints may require cross-module coordination increasing coupling
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts and responsibilities with Teleoperation and Safety Services to reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes cycle time KPI into manageable sub-FRs; selected DP logically addresses these with minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Control logic diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of cycle time optimization DP, with interfaces defined to manage diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and monitoring interface data flows at Vehicle boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of cycle time optimization to Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions to explicitly handle safety constraints and teleoperation latency impacts.
Plan next decomposition level to address teleoperation and safety integration.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and cross-module coordination require refinement to mitigate diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize load phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize travel phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize dump phase cycle time to meet target

revise.why: To clarify and strengthen interface contracts and ownership boundaries with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.

assumptions: Vehicle control systems can be optimized independently per phase
Safety constraints will not overly restrict cycle time improvements
Teleoperation latency is minimal or handled separately
open_risks: Control logic diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Cross-module coordination complexity due to safety constraints
evidence_needed: Interface performance and latency measurements between Vehicle and Teleoperation
Safety constraint impact analysis on cycle time optimization
